steps.title: Compare spread from the center
steps.content: All four data sets are centered at $47$, so the smallest standard deviation comes from the set whose values stay closest to $47$.

steps.title: Check squared deviations
steps.content: For $46,47,47,47,48$ has deviations $-1,0,0,0,1$, so the sum of squared deviations is $$1+0+0+0+1=2$$ The other choices include values farther from $47$, so their sums of squared deviations are larger.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: 46, 47, 47, 47, 48 has the least spread and therefore the smallest standard deviation. D is correct.
